title: rapidly cooked croatian white fish bisque
description: A sour, snappy, one-pot Croatian dish featuring white fish, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a tava-griddle over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned (5 minutes).
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the sliced fennel and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly tender (3 minutes).
Add the white fish fillets and cook, stirring occasionally, until cooked through (4-5 minutes).
Pour in the white wine and fish stock, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly reduced (2-3 minutes).
Stir in the freshly squeezed lemon juice and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the tava-griddle to a bain-marie and cook, covered, for 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Serve the bisque hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
